### Source: [Blade Services](https://swire-re.com/services/blade-services/)
- From inspections to repairs and ongoing maintenance, our end-to-end solutions are tailored to meet your specific needs, supported by an experienced blade engineering team.
- End-to-End Blade Repair Fully functional blades are essential for turbine performance.
- Proactively addressing common damage areas can prevent operational disruptions and costly downtime.
- Swire Renewable Energy offers a complete end-to-end service solution or a bespoke modular approach tailored to your requirements.
- Our capabilities include: Blade Service Evaluation Lightning Damages Laminate Repairs Leading Edge ProtectionWarranty Bonding Repairs Lightning Protection System Maintenance Blade Inspections Gain full visibility of your blade condition with robust, reliable, and repeatable data collection.
- Our inspections empower you to prioritise maintenance effectively, minimise downtime, and maximise performance UAV Inspections Rope Access Inspections Blade Service and Maintenance Proactive blade maintenance prevents revenue loss and extends blade lifespan.
- Swire Renewable Energy services critical blade findings, delivering fast and professional repairs for progressing damages.
- Our preventative maintenance solutions enhance uptime, boost performance, and ensure long-term efficiency.

### Source: [Old Skylotec Power Ascenders](https://swire-re.com/services/blade-services/old-skylotec-power-ascenders/)
- We support customers globally and have a strong presence in the United Kingdom, Europe, South Africa, and Australia.
- We are committed to offering service, quick turnaround, next-day delivery*, and our expertise to our customers.
- Our product categories range from rescue devices, permanent systems to sports.
- We specialise in many industrial applications such as Wind Energy, Power supply / mast work, Maintenance / trade, Rescue at heights, fire departments, high rack, power ascenders training, and permanent systems.
- 01 MAXIMUM VERSATILITY The ActSafe Ascender is a hugely versatile tool that has been successfully deployed in a diverse range of sectors, from renewable energy to construction, entertainment and performing arts.
- 02 INCREASED PRODUCTIVITY Quick and easy to set up, the ActSafe Ascender significantly speeds up rope access and lifting operations.
- For maintenance teams, Actsafe can cut time by up to 30%.
- 03 ERGONOMICS AND SAFETY Not only does working with an ActSafe Ascender reduce fatigue for rope access technicians and personnel; it also reduces physical strain and risk of injury.

### Source: [Swire Renewable Energy Invests In Wind Om Technology Company Bladeinsight](https://swire-re.com/new/swire-renewable-energy-invests-in-wind-om-technology-company-bladeinsight/)
- Global energy services provider, Swire Renewable Energy (SRE) has announced the investment in wind O&M technology start-up, BladeInsight.
- The deal will see SRE obtain a majority shareholding, with options for further future investment.
- Founded in 2015 by André Moura, BladeInsight is a Portugal-based technology company providing the wind sector with cloud-based O&M asset management software and autonomous drone robotic solutions for wind turbine blades.
- Through its licensees, BladeInsight currently services the wind markets in Europe, North and South America.
- This is the first investment in Swire Renewable Energy history that will see a stake investment in a technology company exclusively servicing the renewable energy industry.
- The investment forms part of the long-term strategic development for SRE in its growth path into the renewable energy sector.
- Sabine Weth, VP Offshore Wind for Swire Renewable Energy commented on the announcement, “This investment marks our first in the wind industry and is testament to the commitment and work by André and his team.
- We have been incredibly impressed by the technology and are thrilled that the management team share our vision and strategy for the future development of the company.

### Source: [Swire Renewable Energy Acquires Altitec Blade Services Expanding Blade Services ](https://swire-re.com/new/swire-renewable-energy-acquires-altitec-blade-services-expanding-blade-services-portfolio-and-entering-australian-and-south-african-renewable-energy-service-markets/)
- Altitec Blade Services is a pureplay wind turbine rotor blade inspection, repair and maintenance company operating from offices in the United Kingdom, Germany, Australia and South Africa.
- Altitec offers a comprehensive suite of onshore and offshore wind turbine blade services to wind farm owners, turbine OEM’s, insurers and other participants in the wind power generation industry.
- As part of this strategic acquisition, Swire Renewable Energy will significantly widen its blade repair and maintenance capabilities, and significantly grow its capacity in blade related services, which now include all blade service and maintenance, predictive analysis, engineering, autonomous drone inspections, annual
- Ryan Smith, Chief Executive Officer of Swire Renewable Energy, said “F ollowing the encouraging growth in our blade maintenance division in the last 12 months, we acquired Altitec to strengthen our blade technical capability, increase our operating capacity, and expand our regional footprint.” “There is significant tec
- These are emerging high growth renewable energy services markets where we have significant group experience.
- We will be leveraging our long standing relationships in Africa and Australia to ensure we develop a long term competitive position”,  he said.
- Tom Dyffort, Managing Director of Altitec Blade Services, said “ Altitec has developed a strong position in the wind turbine industry since its inception and has a solid customer base and highly professional and technical blade skillset.
- We are looking forward to integrating with Swire Renewable Energy and optimising our marketing through the larger combined network, enabling us to grow and scale in both onshore and offshore blade repair services internationally” .

### Source: [Gwo Blade Repair Training](https://swire-re.com/academy/gwo-blade-repair-training/)
- This Blade Repair Couse is facilitated by active blade repair technicians, supervisors and engineers, each with more than a decade’s experience in the industry.
- GWO Blade Repair Training from our Academy also includes ActSafe Ascender end-user training, covering safe work practices for battery and petrol-powered ascender systems, and demonstrating the latest rope access methods for wind turbine inspection.
- This is usually done on the Saturday of the 1st week of training and is subject to availability of a Skylotec certified ActSafe trainer.
- The GWO blade repair training course is an entry-level course.
- Delegates are not expected to perform repairs on major structural elements of blades like spars, spar caps and carbon fibre.
- GWO blade repair training Trailing edge repairs up to 1.5m Leading edge repairs up to 1.5m Laminate repairs down to core material Core material replacement up to 200cm² Surface repair to paint and Gelcoat ActSafe End-User Training Course 2.
- ActSafe End-User Training Course Topics Included The key topics addressed in this course include: Composites and Blade Construction Health and Safety Materials, tools and equipment Basic Lamination Blade inspection Lighting Protection system Grinding and sanding skills Laminate repairs and filing Outcome This Blade Rep
- At the end of this course, you will be able to work safely with composite materials while conducting inspection and basic blade repair procedures.

### Source: [Gwo Blade Repair Starter Package](https://swire-re.com/academy/gwo-blade-repair-starter-package/)
- We recommend this to those who are new to the industry.
- We partnered with Belay Rope Access to provide this course package to qualify Blade Technicians at accessible prices compared to booking each course individually.
- This means that some of the training will take place in the training Centre Newhaven, South East England, which can be easily accessed via train.
- What’s Covered Course instruction will cover 4 courses: GWO blade repair training (10 days) GWO Basic Safety Training (4.5 days) IRATA rope access Training (5 days) ActSafe ascenders training (0.5 days) Topics Included The key topics addressed in this package includes: 1.
- IRATA rope access Level 1 Characteristics of a safe system of work, including equipment, legal requirements, objective hazards, risk assessment and method statements, conformity and certification, inspection, emergency considerations and rescue procedures.
- Basic principles of movement on ropes – ascent, descent, changing from ascent to descent, changing from rope to rope, crossing a knot, aid climbing and transversing, passing re-belays and deviations, use of a back-up system, elementary rigging and rope management, elementary rescue.
- GWO Blade Repair training Course delegates will have a foundation of skills to complete repairs to damage on the blade surface, trailing edge, leading edge, sandwich panel and bond line.
- The GWO blade repair training course is intended as an entry level course and therefore the delegate will not be expected to perform repairs in major structural elements of blades like spars, spar caps and carbon fibre.

### Source: [Gwo Blade Repair Career Changer Package](https://swire-re.com/academy/gwo-blade-repair-career-changer-package/)
- With this package, Swire Renewable Energy and Belay Rope Access will provide a specially designed GWO Blade Repair, GWO Full Suite and Refresher BST Modules and IRATA Rope Access (All Levels) training.
- This Blade Repair Career Changer Package will allow delegates to gain essential technical and safety skills, which opens the door to further on-site training or direct employment with leading global blade repair companies.
- We recommend this to those who want to refresh their overall blade repair and safety knowledge.
- The training Centre is located in Newhaven, South East England and can be easily accessed via train.
- What’s Covered Course instruction will cover: GWO blade repair training (10 days) GWO Basic safety Training (5 days) ActSafe ascenders training (0.5 days) Topics Included The key topics addressed in this course include: 1.
- GWO Blade Repair training Course delegates will have a foundation of skills to complete repairs to damage on the blade surface, trailing edge, leading edge, sandwich panel and bond line.
- The GWO blade repair training course is intended as an entry level course and therefore the delegate will not be expected to perform repairs in major structural elements of blades like spars, spar caps and carbon fibre.
- The key topics addressed in this course include Composites and Blade Construction, Health and Safety, Materials, tools and equipment, Basic Lamination, Blade inspection, Lighting Protection system, Grinding and sanding skills, Laminate repairs and filling, Core material replacement up to 200cm², surface repair to paint

### Source: [Gwo Blade Repair Offshore Starter Package](https://swire-re.com/academy/gwo-blade-repair-offshore-starter-package/)
- We recommend this to those who are new to the industry.
- What’s Covered Course instruction will cover: GWO Blade Repair Training GWO Basic Safety Training GWO Advanced Rescue Training GWO Enhanced First Aid IRATA Rope Access Training ActSafe Ascenders Training Topics Included The key topics addressed in this course include: 1.
- GWO Blade Repair Training Course delegates will have a foundation of skills to complete repairs to damage on the blade surface, trailing edge, leading edge, sandwich panel and bond line.
- The GWO blade repair training course is intended as an entry level course and therefore the delegate will not be expected to perform repairs in major structural elements of blades like spars, spar caps and carbon fibre.
- The key topics addressed in this course include Composites and Blade Construction, Health and Safety, Materials, tools and equipment, Basic Lamination, Blade inspection, Lighting Protection system, Grinding and sanding skills, Laminate repairs and filling, Core material replacement up to 200cm², surface repair to paint
- GWO Basic Safety Training All 5 Modules Working at Heights, Sea Survival & Vessel Transfer, First Aid, Manual Handling and Fire Awareness.
- GWO Advanced Rescue Training The Advanced Rescue Training (ART) course is the GWO standard training on emergency rescue missions in WTG environment.
- ART authenticates acquiring the knowledge and skills required to rescue an injured person in each section of a WTG.

- WTG Annual HV Maintenance Regular maintenance of your HV system reduces costs.
- Setting up a maintenance plan for your HV system reduces the risk of unexpected breakdowns of WTG vital components.
- Therefore, compared to dealing with a breakdown, a planned shutdown will always be the cheapest and most effective solution.
- A yearly or half-yearly service agreement with Swire will guarantee a systematic survey of all critical parts that concern the wind turbines grid connection.
- We undertake relevant tests, make the planned replacements and identify irregularities that may cause future problems.
- Everything is documented in reports which give a clear overview of the operational status of the turbine.
- During planned service Swire thoroughly checks the following components: Incoming cables Junction box and HV Switchgear in TP (offshore) HV Switchgear in the turbine Transformer LV cables / Busbar from transformer to main breaker LV main breaker Swire Renewable Energy provides expert support in maintaining and upgradin
- Proper care of the LV main breaker is critical for safeguarding electrical systems and preventing unexpected downtime.

- Our team also conducts troubleshooting in an expedient and safe manner, to get turbines generating in the event of an electrical or mechanical failure, thus reducing generation losses for owner operators.
- Annual Scheduled Maintenance Regular maintenance is the key to protecting your assets and their components
- enhancing safety, increasing production, minimising downtime and ultimately maximising your ROI.

- SRE can provide this as an independent service provider, or as supplementary manpower to those delivering maintenance requirements, offering flexibility and responsiveness to optimum effect.

- When issues such as reduced power output, mechanical failures, or electrical faults occur, Swire technicians utilise diagnostic tools including vibration analysis, temperature checks, and electrical testing.
- Modern turbines, equipped with real-time sensors, enable remote monitoring for early fault detection.

### Source: [Our People](https://swire-re.com/about-us/our-people/)
- Diversity & Inclusion We believe diversity drives innovation and success.
- As part of the John Swire & Sons Group, we are proud to work across cultures, nationalities, ages, and beliefs.
- We are committed to: Providing equal opportunities : Employees are treated with dignity, respect, and fairness.
- Creating an inclusive environment : We celebrate diversity, ensuring no discrimination based on race, religion, gender, orientation, or background.
- Our differences strengthen us, and we recognise the immense value a diverse team brings to our global business.
- Investment in Our People Comprehensive Onboarding : we ensure all new joiners feel supported from day one.
- Ongoing Development : developing our people to be the best of their potential is an ongoing commitment at SRE.
- Our development/career opportunities include apprenticeships, on-the-job training, cross functional training, mentoring and coaching.

### Source: [Careers](https://swire-re.com/careers/)
- We are dedicated to empowering our employees to reach their full potential in a dynamic and evolving renewable energy industry.
- Whether you're a seasoned professional or just starting out, Swire provides an environment that fosters both personal and professional development.
- Our supportive culture ensures you have the resources, mentorship, and opportunities to continuously enhance your skills and build a rewarding career.
- Our growth mindset doesn’t stop with the work we do—it’s deeply ingrained in our approach to developing our people.
- We offer structured learning pathways, leadership development programs, and hands-on project experience, giving you everything you need to thrive.
- Life as a Technician The safety and wellbeing of our people and partners are our top priorities.
- Our commitment to a strong HSE culture ensures the highest standards of safety, quality, and craftsmanship across every project.
- Learn more The foundation for a solid Swire career Safety First We uphold the highest safety standards across all operations, ensuring the well-being of our team members.

### Source: [Life As A Technician](https://swire-re.com/careers/life-as-a-technician/)
- The atmosphere is unique compared to what I’ve experienced before, the city was full of colour.
- There were lots of people dressed up, and a whole week of fireworks.
- I’ve been fascinated by the culture here, everyone has been very friendly, both in day-to-day life in the city as well as my colleagues at work.
- Local people have been very interested in who I am and where I am from, it’s been great to share stories about our different cultures.
- I am currently working on the Greater Changhua site in Taiwan as a HV Technician.
- I wasn’t sure if the work would be different here, I’ve found it is actually very similar to projects I’ve worked on in Europe which has been great.
- The majority of the team’s work has been HV Commissioning, mainly focussing on the testing of switchgear and transformers, as well as first energising.
- The team are great, we are all pulling in the same direction and there is a great mutual respect and trust between everyone.

- There were lots of people dressed up, and a whole week of fireworks.

sections.content:
### Source: [About Us](https://swire-re.com/about-us/)
- Our Mission Our mission is to provide safe, cost-effective and reliable services to energy operations and logistics worldwide on a sustainable basis, enabling our customers to succeed in the ever-changing business environment.
- What We Do We provide a comprehensive range of services, including construction, installation, blade services, electrical and HV expertise, and more.
- Whether it's onshore or offshore, we ensure every project is executed with precision, safety, and an unwavering focus on quality.
- As part of the Swire Group, Swire Renewable Energy belongs to a global network with a rich heritage and diverse business portfolio.
- Swire Group businesses span property, beverages and food chain, aviation, marine, as well as trading and industrial activities.
- With over 100,000 employees world wide, we benefit from a wealth of experience and have adapted best practices from a range of sectors to develop our own standard of safety and operational excellence.
- Know more Our core values Integrity Do the ‘right’ thing No politics Support each other Our word is our bond Humility Respect and learn from each other Be aware of our limitations We all have a place
- be open to others: learn from them We are self-confident but beware vanity and excess pride Endeavour No short cuts Pi
- As an independent company, we are now better positioned to adapt and grow with the rapidly evolving renewable energy market.

### Source: [Hseq](https://swire-re.com/about-us/hseq/)
- We prioritise the safety and wellbeing of our highly skilled workforce, enabling them to operate to the most stringent safety and operational standards.
- We prioritise the quality of our craftsmanship, operating procedures, and safety measures, and are committed to maintaining excellence in every project we undertake.
- The safety and wellbeing of our people, as well as those around us, remain our highest priority.
- Safety Culture   Our leadership promotes an inclusive safety culture aligned with our core values: integrity, humility, endeavour, excellence, and continuity.
-   We prioritise the health, safety, and wellbeing of employees, customers, and partners.
-   Our “Act On What We See” approach focuses on injury and incident prevention, aiming for zero harm.
-   Everyone has the authority to stop work if they observe potential harm to people or the environment.
-   We cultivate an engaged workforce actively contributing to our evolving HSEQ culture.

### Source: [Sustainability](https://swire-re.com/about-us/sustainability/)
- Acting on climate change is our biggest contribution to society and leads us to our target of reaching Net Zero carbon emissions by 2050 or sooner.
- Decarbonisation Strategy  At the top of our agenda is to reduce our impact on climate change, decarbonise our business, and build climate resilience.
- It is anchored in our overall approach to sustainability and a key area of our sustainability strategy and related targets.
- Our ESG policies additionally accelerate the climate change agenda and frames disciplines of decarbonisation across business activities.
- Although our services contribute to a more sustainable future, we need to continuously drive down our carbon footprint.
- We support the Paris Agreement and commit to limit global warming to 1.5°C with our target of reaching Net Zero by 2050 or sooner for our carbon emissions.
- Aims Reduce our carbon footprint Turn today’s waste into a resource for tomorrow Use water responsibly and sustainably Protect biodiversity under our influence Source materials responsibly and sustainably Promote equal opportunities, diversity, and inclusion Engage with stakeholders and anchor sustainable development a
- Climate – Decarbonise our business and build climate resilience Waste – Turn today’s waste into tomorrow’s resource Water – Use and manage water responsibly People – Foster a corporate culture that is accessible, inclusive and safe Communities – Position our community initiative as part of our core business value Learn

### Source: [Code Of Ethics](https://swire-re.com/about-us/code-of-ethics/)
- Compliance with Swire Renewable Energy’s Code of Ethics is an essential part of our global operations, and as part of the wider Swire Group, we at Swire Renewable Energy are proud of this reputation.
- Our core values of integrity, humility, endeavour, excellence and continuity underpin everything we do as a business
- and as these values show, we are dedicated to operating in a fair and ethical manner across our business.
- Code of Ethics At Swire Renewable Energy, we are committed to acting fairly, professionally and with integrity.
- Our reputation for fair dealing, integrity and doing the right thing depends on the highest standards, and complying with our code of ethics is vital in achieving this.
- The reputation for honesty and fair dealing, which we have gained over the last 40+ years, is something we are incredibly proud of.
- Our code of ethics states our position on a range of important topics including: Bribery and corruption (aligned with the UK Bribery Act 2010) Procurement principles Facilitation payments Gifts, hospitality and expenses Political and charitable donations Competition and anti-trust Business and personal relationships Re
- At its heart, it showcases our commitment to uphold laws relevant to countering bribery and corruption in all jurisdictions in which we operate, and prohibits the offering, giving, receiving or soliciting of any financial or other advantage in order to influence other people, whether directly or indirectly.
- It also reiterates our zero-tolerance approach to bribery and provides a clear reporting line for any suspicions.

### Source: [Swire Renewable Energy Acquires Rope Access Sverige Ab](https://swire-re.com/new/swire-renewable-energy-acquires-rope-access-sverige-ab/)
- The acquisition supports SRE’s continued growth in onshore and offshore wind inspection, repair and maintenance markets in the UK and Europe and significantly enhances its expertise and capacity in wind turbine blade repairs.
- The acquisition builds on SRE’s previous acquisitions in the last 12 months; WPS in Sweden, Altitec Blade Services in the UK, and Obelisk in South Africa and Ireland.
- Rope Access Sverige AB provides a range of core services, including blade inspections and repairs, on-site blade operations management, and a variety of safety trainings.
- These capabilities will enhance SRE’s presence in its core Nordic, European and UK markets.
- Ryan Smith, Chief Executive Officer of Swire Renewable Energy, said: “Acquiring Rope Access reinforces our determination to develop a world-class independent blade inspection, repair and maintenance division within our growing wind farm maintenance and services business across the Nordics, Europe and the UK.
- Like SRE, Rope Access Sverige has a safety-first culture, and we are pleased to welcome their highly skilled technicians and management team into SRE.” “Rope Access Sverige provides synergy and significant scale given our recent acquisitions of Altitec Blade Services in the UK, and also an expanded service footprint in
- Niklas Andersson, Chief Executive Officer of Rope Access Sverige, said: “Rope Access has developed a strong position in the wind turbine industry with highly professional and skilled blade technicians as well as training instructors.
- We look forward to integrating with the SRE business to increase the competitiveness of SRE’s expanded service offering, which will benefit existing Rope Access customers.” About Swire Renewable Energy Swire Renewable Energy is a growth focused renewable energy inspection, repair and maintenance service provider.

### Source: [Announcement From Ceo Covid 19](https://swire-re.com/new/announcement-from-ceo-covid-19/)
- As a company and employer, we have a great responsibility to ensure that we are taking the right measures to protect our employees, customers and partners and avoid further spread of the virus, while at the same time doing whatever we can to ensure business continuity and minimize disruption of our services.
- During our 40 years of operations around the world, we have developed solid plans and procedures to respond to a crisis such as this.
- However, the magnitude and impact of this pandemic is difficult to predict and we need to constantly adapt to minimize the impact and secure continuity.
- Swire Energy Services is following the global development of the COVID-19 situation closely through official channels and in close collaboration with our local management teams around the world so we can quickly adjust our own preventive and contingency plans should this be necessary.
- We have activated our Emergency Response Teams who are in regular communication with each other to ensure consistency and continuity of operations in compliance with recommendations from the World Health Organisation and local authorities.
- I want to personally assure you we are doing everything possible to keep our operations and services available without challenging the safety of our own and your employees while reducing the risk of further spread.
- This may temporarily impact the way we are operating or the way we are collaborating with you.
- Our local teams are committed to working with you on the changes necessary and I hope that together we can find the best possible solutions.

### Source: [Company Update On Covid 19](https://swire-re.com/new/company-update-on-covid-19/)
- We have a focus on keeping  our bases open to support the needs of our customers, whilst closely following the guidance set out by local government.
- As an organisation, our tagline, Servicing The Energy World, seems more important now than ever as we endeavour to ensure that our customers can rely on us to support them when they need it the most, and we are addressing the ongoing changes with a dynamic approach.
- Our Global and Local Emergency Response Plans have been actioned, with the Executive and Local Senior Management teams meeting on an ongoing basis with a focus on responding to the changing environment, protecting our personnel and ensuring our customers are supported.
- Our Teams Our focus will always be on the safety and health of our people, and we are operating our global operations in compliance to local government guidelines, implementing the necessary controls and risk mitigations in relation to COVID-19.
- In most locations, where possible, office personnel are working from home and we have invested in additional equipment and infrastructure to ensure minimum disruption to normal business operations.
- Yard personnel continue to support the industry and a number of measures have been implemented following a full risk assessment of the operation.
- These measures include social distancing and staggered shift start times to minimise contact with each other.
- Oil & Gas and Renewables is seen as a key sector and, as such, the personnel are classed as key workers to support the supply chain, and we will continue to play our role to support this industry.

### Source: [Swire Conducts Statutory Inspections On An Offshore Windfarm Substation](https://swire-re.com/new/swire-conducts-statutory-inspections-on-an-offshore-windfarm-substation/)
-   Challenge The requirement included various MPI and visual inspections to be completed on the wind farm's transformer platform.
- Essential maintenance to both the lifeboat davit and the ladder systems used to access the wind farm was also carried out.
- This was our first venture into the offshore wind sector, and the customer trusted that our 40+ years of experience in oil and gas would allow us to apply our knowledge and experience of the offshore environment to complete this project to a high standard.
-   Solution We dispatched one of our skilled engineers to the wind farm to carry out the work required which included rescue davit modification, assembly, inspection and testing.
- The modification included stripping down the davit, modifying it and reassembling, all of which was conducted on the offshore platform.
- The engineer also carried out an access ladder survey and MPI inspection.
- In addition, a proof-load test and inspection was carried out, which included a series of bolt torqueing tests.
- Following the survey, we produced a document which included a risk assessment and method statements.

-   Challenge The requirement included various MPI and visual inspections to be completed on the wind farm's transformer platform.
- Essential maintenance to both the lifeboat davit and the ladder systems used to access the wind farm was also carried out.
- This was our first venture into the offshore wind sector, and the customer trusted that our 40+ years of experience in oil and gas would allow us to apply our knowledge and experience of the offshore environment to complete this project to a high standard.
-   Solution We dispatched one of our skilled engineers to the wind farm to carry out the work required which included rescue davit modification, assembly, inspection and testing.
- The modification included stripping down the davit, modifying it and reassembling, all of which was conducted on the offshore platform.
- The engineer also carried out an access ladder survey and MPI inspection.
- In addition, a proof-load test and inspection was carried out, which included a series of bolt torqueing tests.
